mainText:
**U.S. Strikes Tanker Attempting to Violate U.S. Blockade**
U.S. Central Command announced Wednesday that it fired on a Palau-flagged oil tanker on Tuesday in the Gulf of Oman after the vessel attempted to breach the U.S. blockade of Iranian ports. The tanker’s crew included 24 Indian nationals, with the Indian government reporting that 21 crew members had been rescued, while three were confirmed dead. Following the incident, the Indian government summoned the deputy head of the U.S. mission in Delhi as a formal protest.

- The U.S. military struck multiple targets in Iran on Wednesday, with operations continuing overnight against locations in Tehran, Bandar Abbas, and other sites near the Strait of Hormuz.
- In retaliation, Iran launched strikes on Bahrain, Kuwait, and Jordan, resulting in one fatality at the Kuwait International Airport. The Jordanian military reported that it intercepted 20 missiles aimed at a U.S. base in the country.
- President Donald Trump stated in an interview that he was close to ordering strikes on Iranian power plants and bridges.

**Russian Military Officer Killed in Moscow Carbombing**
Media reports indicated Wednesday that Col. Damir Davydov, a senior Russian military officer, was killed by a car bomb early Tuesday in Balashikha, near Moscow. Davydov was head of Russia’s artillery and missile ammunition supply directorate. Although he was reportedly still alive when pulled from his burning vehicle, he succumbed to his injuries later. Russian authorities have confirmed the explosion but have not identified any victims. Another car bomb incident occurred in southwestern Moscow the same day, but no casualties were reported.

- Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky announced that Ukrainian long-range missiles hit a drone and missile production facility in Cheboksary, Russia, over 560 miles from the front lines.
- Ukrainian officials confirmed attacks on a port in the Russian-occupied city of Mariupol, an oil tanker in the Black Sea, and an oil refinery in Samara. Three people were injured in the Cheboksary attack.
- The Ukrainian air force claimed to have intercepted 181 of the 207 drones launched by Russia overnight, while Russia claimed to have downed 326 Ukrainian drones.

**U.S. Inflation Increases**
According to the Consumer Price Index report from the Department of Labor, prices rose 4.2% in May compared to the previous year, exceeding April's 3.8% increase. This marks the highest inflation rate since April 2023. The rise in prices was largely attributed to increasing energy costs, which accounted for more than 60% of the monthly increase. Core inflation, excluding food and energy, was reported at 2.9%, slightly higher than April's 2.8%.

- Airline fares rose 2.7% in May, nearly 27% higher compared to last year.
- For the second consecutive month, inflation outpaced wage gains, with real hourly earnings dropping by 0.7% compared to last May.
- Average gas prices have decreased to $4.15 a gallon from $4.56 last month.

**Academic Scores Recover From Pandemic**
Average reading scores for 9-year-olds, who were preschool-age during COVID-19, rose by 4 points in both reading and math compared to 2022, according to the National Assessment of Educational Progress results released Wednesday. For the first time since the pandemic, reading scores are statistically similar to 2020 levels, while math scores are still 4 points below. The gains were notably among lower-performing students and those from lower-income households. However, 13-year-olds showed no significant improvement since 2023.

- Just 37% of 9-year-olds and 14% of 13-year-olds reported reading for fun “almost every day” in 2025, down from peaks of 53% and 35%, respectively, over the last 40 years.

**World Cup Starts**
The 2026 FIFA World Cup soccer tournament kicks off today, featuring Mexico against South Africa in Mexico City and South Korea facing Czechia in Guadalajara. The group stage will conclude on June 27, with the top 32 teams advancing to the knockout rounds, culminating in the final on July 19. The U.S. team will begin play on Friday against Paraguay in Los Angeles. Spain is favored to win, followed by France, Argentina, and England, while Cape Verde, Curaçao, Jordan, and Uzbekistan will make their debut in the tournament.
